title: Queen's Indian Defense: Classical
summary: Learn the Queen's Indian Defense, where Black fights White's central pawn with piece pressure.
description: The Queen's Indian Defense is one of Black's most solid responses to 1.d4. By playing ...b6 and ...Bb7, Black fights White's central pawn with the bishop. This tutorial covers the Classical setup and typical plans.

variations.classical: 4...Ba6 - The Classical Queen's Indian with Ba6
variations.modern: 4...Bb7 - The Modern Queen's Indian, playing in the center

objectives: Understand the Queen's Indian structure: why the bishop on b7 is powerful
Learn why Black fights the center with pieces, not pawns
Master typical plans: ...d5, ...c5, or ...e5 depending on position
plans: White's plan: Build a central pawn majority and use the bishop pair
Black's plan: Play ...d5, ...c5, or ...e5 to challenge the center
The move ...Ba6 is the main line, threatening b5
White should maintain central pressure before expanding
commonMistakes: Do not think the Queen's Indian is boring - there are many strategic ideas
Avoid playing too passively - keep the initiative
Do not underestimate Black's activity - the bishop on b7 is powerful
White should prepare e4 carefully before pushing
completionTitle: Strategic position with chances for both
completionSummary: The Queen's Indian is a solid defense. You've learned the main ideas and can navigate the position.

steps.title: Claim central space
steps.instruction: Play d4 to control the center.
steps.coaching: The Queen's Indian is one of Black's most solid defenses.
steps.hint: Push the queen pawn two squares.

steps.title: Black develops the knight
steps.coaching: Black attacks d4 and prepares the Queen's Indian.

steps.title: Challenge the center
steps.coaching: This is the standard setup against the Queen's Indian.

steps.title: Black plays e6
steps.instruction: Black plays e6, preparing the Queen's Indian.
steps.coaching: Black is setting up the Queen's Indian structure.

steps.title: Develop the knight
steps.instruction: Move Nf3, developing toward the center.

steps.title: The Queen's Indian move
steps.instruction: Black plays b6, preparing the bishop development.
steps.coaching: This is the Queen's Indian! Black will develop the bishop to b7.

steps.title: Prevent ...b5
steps.coaching: This move prevents Black's typical ...b5 break.

steps.title: The Classical bishop
steps.coaching: This is the Classical Queen's Indian! The bishop attacks c4.

steps.title: Support the c4 pawn
steps.coaching: White strengthens the c4 pawn and prepares development.

steps.title: Black exchanges the bishop
steps.coaching: Black exchanges the bishop, simplifying the position.

steps.title: White recaptures
steps.coaching: White accepts the exchange, getting the king to safety.
steps.hint: Capture on f1 with the king.

steps.title: Black's central break
steps.instruction: Play d5 to challenge the center.
steps.coaching: This is Black's typical break in the Queen's Indian.

steps.title: White exchanges
steps.instruction: Capture on d5 with the c-pawn.

steps.title: Black recaptures
steps.coaching: Black gets an isolated pawn but keeps piece activity.
